paper_map.title: Low-density nano-bainitic Al–C steels
paper_map.abstract_summary: The bainitic transformation kinetics and mechanical properties of five medium-to-high Al steels with varying C contents and alloying elements (Ti, Ni) were investigated under different austenitizing/austempering parameters. In high-Al (≥6 wt%) grades, transformation time ranged from 1600 s to 43,000 s depending on composition and temperature, while a lower-C, moderate-Al steel exhibited the fastest kinetics (700–1000 s). The ultimate tensile strength ranged from 1315 to 1600 MPa, with Ti-microalloyed steel achieving the highest average total elongation (10%) under optimal heat treatment. The study highlights the role of alloy composition and microstructural features, including retained austenite stability, precipitation, and martensite formation, in governing the transformation behavior and strength–ductility balance.
paper_map.research_objective: To clarify the influence of high C–Al contents, Ti microalloying, and heat treatment parameters on bainitic transformation kinetics and mechanical properties in low-density nano-bainitic steels.
material_systems: Low-density nano-bainitic Al–C steels: Fe-C-Al-Si-Mn-Mo-(B) system
Ti-microalloyed variant S4 with Ni addition
Low C–Al alloyed S5 containing 0.60% C, 3.92% Al, 0.78% Ni
Key phases: bainite, retained austenite, polygonal ferrite, martensite, (Fe,M)xCy, TiMoC, AlN
main_process_variables: Alloy composition (C, Al, Mn, Si, Mo, B, Ti, Ni)

sample_aliases.description: High-C (0.9–1.0 wt%), high-Al (≥6 wt%) steel, B microalloyed
Similar to S1: high-C, high-Al, B microalloyed
Steel with 8.08 wt% Al, high C, B microalloyed
Ti-microalloyed steel, slightly lower Al and reduced C, with 0.78% Ni to suppress polygonal ferrite
Low C (0.60 wt%), moderate Al (3.92 wt%), with 0.78% Ni addition

key_sections.description: Background on low-density nano-bainitic steels, role of Al, C, and microalloying, and motivation for studying high-C–Al compositions.
Describes the design rationale for five steel grades, sample preparation, heat treatment cycles, and characterization techniques (LOM, SEM/EDX, XRD, EBSD).

subsections: 3.1 Determination of TA and Ms temperatures
3.4 Microscopic investigation (incl. quantitative analysis, TTP precipitation, and dedicated discussions for each steel group)
key_sections.description: Presents experimental data on transformation temperatures, kinetics, tensile properties, microstructural observations, and precipitation analysis, with separate discussions for ≥6% Al steels, Ti-microalloyed steel, and the lower C–Al steel.
Summarizes the comparative findings on bainitic transformation kinetics and tensile behavior across the five low-density Al–C steels, supported by microscopy and simulation results.
